title: Through the Kazhagam's Coming Victory, Let Us Prove That Even a Small Mustard Seed Never Loses Its Heat!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The Tamil section below is a corrected reading copy; the canonical Tamil chapter remains untouched. All five source pages were checked for the fundraising total, quoted appeal, repeated passages, proverb, closing exhortation, and signature. The repeated fundraising appeal is present in the printed source and is therefore preserved. Clearly visible OCR character and word-boundary errors were corrected only in the reading copy, and the source confirms the closing date as 27 December 2015. The visually verified printed Tamil title is retained without grammatical modernisation.
paragraphs: Some months have passed since I wrote to you on 12 March 2015 under the title “Let Election Funds Pour In to Protect Our Principles.” Addressing all of you with affection, I began:

> “The general election to the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly will be held in April or May 2016. The time of deliverance is approaching from all the suffering the people of this State have endured over five years.”
I then asked you to contribute substantially to the Kazhagam's election fund.

By yesterday, the money you had given with love and contributed with enthusiasm had grown, drop by drop into a great flood, reaching ₹21,93,96,534. Compared with the enormous funds the ruling party in Tamil Nadu has amassed through many means, this ₹22 crore may look like a tiny mustard seed. But you, the Kazhagam brethren who gave it, know well that my fellow brethren and I rejoice in this fund in the spirit of the old saying, “Though the mustard seed may be small, it never loses its heat.” Our Treasurer, all the Kazhagam's active workers, and I take pride in having received an election fund of this size.
But, my brethren, is pride alone enough to achieve success? It is not. It is nowhere near enough.

Whether the coming elections are to the Legislative Assembly or to Parliament, large numbers of our Kazhagam members must take their places in both Houses. Your work, my brethren, must be directed accordingly. You must not perform election work splendidly but remain thoughtless and unconcerned about raising the election fund. If you show still greater enthusiasm in that task too, I have complete confidence that, with the election funds you have given and will yet give, we shall secure victories in the coming elections that will make everyone rejoice.

The people now have an opportunity to recognise the Government ruling Tamil Nadu. We, those who are neutral, and the people of the State generally see directly and grieve over the suffering endured every day by the poor and the humble. In these circumstances, both you and I understand that the ₹22 crore election fund you have so far collected will not be enough even for the most essential expenses of facing the election.
Therefore, however much more you can contribute within your means, even if it consists only of silver coins, I shall receive it as though it were gold.

Continue the work of contributing to the election fund and encourage me still further. Put into action your feeling that “We shall build and protect the Kazhagam through our labour; we shall push resistance aside.” More than this election fund itself, I will receive even greater joy if you pledge to work together to strengthen Dravidian consciousness.

Our Kazhagam is a great movement of working people labouring to determine Tamil Nadu's destiny. It was created by Thanthai Periyar and Perarignar Anna to protect the Dravidian people. From that day to this, we have carried it in our hands like a sledgehammer to defeat the enemies of our society and used it as a weapon in a righteous struggle to protect Tamil society.

This incomparable movement, this movement of dignity, possesses neither a great army nor sacks of money. Yet it labours without pause to remove the political and social degradation of Dravidians and enable them always to rise. Its young people, women, students and members of many sections are its precious gems and its soldiers. This camp must win a great victory and demonstrate in action our conviction that it is an unequalled fortress of dignity which no one can bring down.

In a democracy, electoral victories are the identifying signs and stars of hope through which that conviction is proved. Therefore, my beloved brethren, dearer to me than life itself, though the election fund you are contributing may appear modest as a number, I tell you firmly that it increases every day in my heart the confidence I place in you.

Think of the vast funds our opponents have amassed through all manner of deeds by the back door. Only a few months remain before the election, an election that will determine the destiny of every Tamil. Realising that each of us must perform our duty in it, you must work with concern both to contribute more and more to the election fund and to explain to the public, in every possible way, this Government's corruption, irregularities and decay.

Let this resolve suffer no setback even for a day. Continue contributing to the election fund and encourage me still further. Put into action your feeling that “We shall build and protect the Kazhagam through our labour; we shall push resistance aside.” More than this election fund itself, I will receive even greater joy if you pledge to work together to strengthen Dravidian consciousness.

Udanpirappē, because I have said this, you must not console yourselves that what you have already given is enough. As I wrote earlier, think of the enormous funds our opponents have amassed by all kinds of means through the back door. Only a few months remain before the election. It will determine the destiny of every Tamil, and every one of us must perform our duty. Realising all this, you must work diligently to contribute more and more to the election fund and to explain to the public in every possible way this Government's corruption, irregularities and decay.

I know very well that at every election you, my brethren, stand with me and give generously as soon as I ask, never refusing by saying you have nothing. If I do not ask you at this moment, whom else can I approach? I am not accustomed to seeking election funds from the wealthy, industrial magnates or those who possess many crores of rupees. Udanpirappē, it is you whom I ask again and again.

And you continue to give without fail. But when you consider the money power accumulated by our opponent, you must realise that what you have given now is nowhere near enough. Remember that only a few days remain. With the spirit of those who return carrying an entire bundle when asked to bring something cut from the field, you must from this very day show determination both in bringing substantial funds to add to the Kazhagam's fund and in carrying out election work without slackening.
Whenever time permits, I shall continue writing to you from time to time about election work.
Through the victory the Kazhagam is going to win, let us prove that even when the mustard seed is small, its heat does not diminish!
With affection,
M.K.
27 December 2015
